In the multi-level world, where I had my earliest experience, we had to do a lot of this. I learned real quick there was no point in recruiting somebody and sending them home without this preparation. Because there's more people trying to get them out than there is me trying to get them in. And they're sleeping with some of them and they're working with some of them every day and they're saying, you've got to prepare them. Here's what's going to happen. And you've got to negatively characterize all those people. And they're going to descend on you like vultures picking at meat. And they are going to try and drag you away from success because of their own past conditioning and purposes. And so there's a big long wrap about this. There's a particular audio cassette they go home with that reinforces that particular message. So now, when their buddy at work says these 18 words about why this is the dumb... They've already been told he was going to say that. They've already been told not only is he going to say it, but here's why he's saying it. And you can't trust him about this and you have to separate yourself from him. So they're right about that. There's a firewall between one side of this equation and the other. 98, page 98. First, have privileged information. Communication within this group is intimate, extensive, and frequent. Well, there's our businesses. It's all about frequent, extensive, and the best part, the part we're really talking about here today, is intimate. So it can't just be the business communication. It can't just be the focus on the clinical, the technical, the mechanical. It has to arrive at a level of intimacy because they have influence over them to maintain their interest and attention and fascination. We are in a sense competing with other forces that have a level of intimacy with them. So we got to have it. So there's a difference between external and internal communication. And so, like external, for me, would be the books, would be something like Renegade Millionaire Magazine, articles that go on other people's newsletters. Internal would be the newsletters, the weekly faxes, the blog. And so there's some commonality between the two, but there's also a lot of difference between the two. I talk differently to you once we have a intimate relationship than I do when we are meeting for the first time, say, through a book. And you need to do the same thing with your audiences. 99, creating a parallel social universe. This is all about place. So you separate people from their previous existences by luring them completely into a parallel social universe with its own rituals, relationship structures, and experiences. And as an observation, the thing that a lot of information marketers, by the way, ourselves included, dropped the ball on a little bit on this list is the ritual stuff. It's actually pretty important. And we used to do a lot of it in the human potential business. So the prescribed ritual of what you do when you get up in the morning. So you probably do more of this than anybody in the room because you give them exercise regiments of exactly how many minutes they're supposed to spend doing this and they're supposed to stand on their head for X number of minutes and in the corner facing east, not facing west. That sort of thing. Most of the rest of us don't do enough of this very well. And as I was preparing for this, I realized how much of this I used to do and how little of it I now do. The whole issuance of awards and the desire to earn this award and this award and this award and this award through behavior. I'll give you an extreme example of that. And now I have candlebell certifications. The first four or five hours of the day, people have to carry their candlebell wherever they go, even if they go to the bathroom to bond with their candlebell. It's actually taken from a military strategy, a military training strategy, that's part of exactly what we're talking about. Bonding with the bell. You ever crack a urine on the man? You ever saw him too? Cracking the urine on the man. Well, laugh all you want, but you know, he's got it right. So there's rituals, there's relationship structures in their experiences that are very different from what? Do you recommend group rituals? Yeah, as well as singles. Really what we want to do is reach into and be omnipresent in their lives, right? So, like, one of the things that we did to human potential business, which is right from religion, is the required daily reading. So the notebook with the reading to be done first thing in the morning. It's why you give them things to carry around with them. So everybody uses the same day planner, right? And everybody schleps around the big brown notebook with the buckle on it and uses the red pen and the blue pen and the green pen. So like we did things like, so like we taught them all to always park their car backwards. You always backed into the parking space. The reason for that is because you want to be mentally thinking forward. So everybody goes out and gets in their car and starts going everywhere they're trying to go and reverse, right? And you want to go forward. And so we trained everybody to back their cars into parking lots. Well, every time they backed their car in, what were they thinking of? Us. So we had reinforcement, five, six, seven, eight, nine, ten times a day, right? Through that simple device. Green flare pens. God forbid we should catch you writing with anything but a green flare pen because it's the color of money and it writes bold. And if you saw them, you'd walk over and took it away from them and threw it on the ground and told them to go get a green flare pen. And pretty soon everybody's writing with green flare pens. Well, every time they write with a green flare pen, what is it? It's reinforcement, right? Start every day with a hundred dollar bill in your pocket and break the hundred dollar. So every time now the guys make a change during days thinking about us. And so this is ritualistic reach into their daily experience. The group stuff at meetings is fine, but you don't have them in meetings. You know, and we now in our businesses for the most part do less of it than I did in that life. We only see them once or twice or three times or four times a year, like if you got them at a coaching program, right? But what you really want is to be omnipresent. You want to move your place to them, if you will. It's why stuff, apparel, mouse pads, posters, the notebook. It's why all that stuff is important because it's about moving our place into their place, right? And so like we also taught sleep learning, right? So that the last thing we had them doing at night was going and getting one of our tapes, putting the tape in the sleep machine, putting the pillow speaker under the pillow, right? I think it was sleep learning works or not. It happens to work to a certain degree, but it's irrelevant. What's really important is they end every day by dealing with our stuff and thinking about us, right? It does happen to work a little bit, but okay, page 100, coded communication. This is really important because this is red room, blue room, verbalized. And so this is where lingo works to your advantage in that it's the version of the secret handshake at the lodge, which at least goes back to Fred Flintstone, if not before, right? Is that there ought to be some language that only makes sense to us that outsiders don't get. And if they hear it, they want to know what the hell you're talking about, right? If you've ever been around, if you've ever had to work in a place where there were a couple of employees who spoke a language you don't speak and they kept conversing with each other in French or Spanish or whatever they were conversing in, it bugs the hell out of you, right? You want to know what they're saying, right? And you feel like an outsider around them, even if they're a minority in the room, right? And nobody likes to feel like an outsider. Everybody wants to be an insider. Everybody wants to belong. So the physical manifestation of this at meetings, MLM meetings, cult meetings, human potential meetings is, the guest is seeing everybody else communicate in this language that they only understand part of. And now it's created a physical red room, blue room. They want to be over here with complete understanding. And so the coded communication, the language of your own. And so NLP is a language of its own. Tony created a language of his own. It's because he comes from the MLM world where this is very common, the human potential world where this is very common. And so it doesn't even have to be incomprehensible. It just has to be sort of a repetitive part of the language that you have your internal communication between you and everybody who's a member, customer or whatever. So that there's a level of language and communication going on that the outside world doesn't get. And if an outsider hears that they need it deciphered, they want to know more about what that is. And every one of these movements has that as a commonality. And so you've got to give it some thought.
